checks.finding: All five tasks are high or critical in line with their protected enforcement-chain and workflow-selection boundaries.
Every high/critical task requires RED/GREEN evidence and an independent quality-gate report; T-005 additionally requires cross-model consensus, a signed bundle, and a distinct second approver.
Each task remains scoped to one source issue and one coherent protected candidate set.

T-004 does not make the policy-selection order observable: it needs explicit fixtures that default/profile/--lite scan before lite selection while --full bypasses scanning, opaque/unreadable lite-spec input fails before writing, and ship fails full-required if the task block or requirements input is absent.
The unit, PowerShell, cross-runtime parity, and workflow-conformance test types match their stated acceptance criteria.
Each protected change has a concrete human re-copy rollback procedure.
No planned protected target is independently modified by two tasks; T-005 consumes the prior staged candidates as a single canonical inventory operation.

T-005's exact protected target inventory contains T-003's check-contract.ps1 and T-004's policy, checkers, and skill targets, but it depends only on T-001/T-002. It must wait for T-003 and T-004 before generating/binding the full inventory.
The two behavior fixes require a captured failing fixture before staging and preserve a focused regression path.

findings.finding: Add TEST-007 through TEST-009 assertions for full-policy selection ordering, pre-write unreadable/opaque lite input failure, and missing ship task-block/requirements full-required failure.
Add T-003 and T-004 to T-005 Depends On and Blockers before it binds the protected canonical target inventory.
